title: Tagging / feature-schema profile (HOT presets)
context: open-map-gaps packs map public infrastructure (roads, buildings, health facilities, schools, water/sanitation points, markets, places of worship, administrative names) using the standard HOT/Missing Maps feature set. Consistent OSM tagging is essential: wrong tags waste validator time or pollute the commons. The HOT presets (used in iD, JOSM, RapiD) define the canonical tag schema for humanitarian mapping. This task produces the project-wide tagging profile, derived from HOT presets, that every pack's feature schema references.
objective: Define and commit a tagging / feature-schema profile based on HOT presets, covering the in-scope feature classes, so every pack references a consistent, OSM-valid tag schema.
acceptanceCriteria: A committed tagging profile covers all in-scope feature classes: roads/paths, buildings (footprints, no occupant data), health facilities, schools, water/sanitation points, markets, places of worship as public landmarks, and administrative/place names from authorized sources.
Each feature class records the canonical OSM tags (key=value pairs), the HOT preset reference (iD/JOSM/RapiD preset name or URL), and validation rules.

Profile explicitly prohibits tagging occupant names, household composition, individuals' locations, religion/ethnicity/health status of residents, or any re-identifying personal information on building or POI features.
Profile is derived from and cites the HOT preset set and OSM wiki tagging conventions; any non-standard tag is flagged for community review.
Reviewed by the technical reviewer; referenced by template-004 and all subsequent packs.
output: A committed tagging / feature-schema profile document listing canonical OSM tags, HOT preset references, and validation rules for each in-scope feature class, with explicit PII prohibitions, ready to be embedded in every task pack.
